items.title: SCIB names CEO Michael Tan as executive director
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Sarawak Consolidated Industries Bhd (SCIB) has appointed its chief executive officer Michael Tan Thian Zhi as executive director. In a statement, SCIB said the appointment follows Tan's elevation to chief executive officer on May 29. The company said Tan brings a combination of engineering expertise, project execution experience and strategic management skills.

items.title: 200 join Umno ahead of Johor election, says candidate
items.summary: PONTIAN: About 200 members from several political parties, led by a former Bersatu leader, have joined Umno, citing confidence in the party and Barisan Nasional's leadership ahead of the Johor election. The group was led by former Pontian Bersatu division vice-chief Hairul Azwani A. Karim. Hairul said the defectors included former members of Bersatu, Pas, PKR and Amanah from Sungai Terus and Kayu Ara Pasong.

items.title: Tyre on NSE kills policeman, injures three
items.summary: BUTTERWORTH: A 40-year-old policeman was killed after his motorcycle struck a tyre on the North-South Expressway near Seberang Jaya early today. Penang police chief Datuk Dennis Lim Kwang Keng said police received a report on the fatal crash at Km140.6 of the North-South Expressway northbound about 6am. "The impact caused the driver to lose control before the vehicle veered onto the left road shoulder.

items.title: Over 100 police, family members pay last respects to cop killed in crash
items.summary: SEBERANG JAYA: More than 100 police officers, friends and family members gathered at the Seberang Jaya Hospital Forensic Unit today to pay their respects to a policeman who was killed in a road crash. The body arrived at the forensic unit about 8.30am. Earlier, the 40-year-old policeman, who was attached to the Perlis contingent police headquarters, was killed after his motorcycle struck a tyre lying on the North-South Expressway near Seberang Jaya about 6am.

items.title: Bukit Aman moves to profile drivers, companies and vehicles in drug- and drunk-driving crackdown
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 1 — The Bukit Aman Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department (JSPT) will develop a profiling system involving drivers, companies and commercial vehicles to strengthen efforts to curb drug- and alcohol-impaired driving. Bukit Aman JSPT Director Datuk Seri Muhammed Hasbullah Ali said the initiative is being refined as part of proposals to tighten enforcement, including ensuring companies that employ such drivers are also held accountable for offences committed. “Nearly 90 per cent of those detained were commercial vehicle drivers, involving 121 lorry drivers and 14 others,” he said.

items.title: MBSA: LRT3 pedestrian walkways were required under 2019 approvals for Prasarana and developers
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, July 1 — The Shah Alam City Council (MBSA) today said pedestrian walkways linking LRT3 stations to surrounding developments had been incorporated into development plans approved as early as 2019, following public concerns over incomplete access routes after the line began operations. In a statement, MBSA said it had required pedestrian walkways connecting the stations to nearby commercial developments, public facilities and activity centres as part of the planning approval process for the LRT3 project. “Accordingly, when approving the development plans for the LRT3 stations in 2019, MBSA imposed conditions requiring the provision of pedestrian walkways connecting the stations to surrounding activity centres, commercial developments and public facilities,” it said.

items.title: MARKET PULSE PM JULY 1, 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia ended lower, giving up earlier gains as late-session selling pressure dragged the benchmark index to its intraday low. This reflects a cautious tone among investors amid a lack of fresh market catalysts. That wraps up today's Market Pulse.

items.title: Sultan Selangor credits Najib, thanks Anwar over LRT3
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah is pleased with the Shah Alam LRT3 line. The Selangor sultan expressed his gratitude to and congratulated Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, and gave credit to former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak. On Anwar, Sultan Sharafuddin acknowledged the role the prime minister had played in ensuring the continuation and completion of the Shah Alam LRT3 project.

items.title: Johor polls: Onn Hafiz rejects uneven development claims, cites state economic transformation plan for balanced growth
items.summary: MUAR, July 1 — Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi has dismissed claims that development in the state is uneven and has forced residents to migrate elsewhere, stressing that development across all districts is guided by a comprehensive plan. Onn Hafiz, who is also Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman, explained that under the Johor Economic Transformation Plan (JETP), the state’s development framework is planned comprehensively, with strategic emphasis placed on equitably addressing the unique socioeconomic priorities of each district. Also present was BN candidate for the Maharani state seat, Datuk Ashari Md Sarip.

items.title: Ringgit ends lower as Fed rate hike concerns lift US dollar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit ended lower against the US dollar today as expectations of a United States (US) Federal Reserve (Fed) rate hike continued to weigh on regional market sentiment. At 6pm, the local note depreciated to 4.0920/0965 versus the greenback from 4.0825/0870 at Tuesday's close. He said the Fed rate hike expectation had continued to support the strength of the US dollar, despite lower crude oil prices.

items.title: Marina claims DAP practised double standards on Najib
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Former Skudai assemblywoman Marina Ibrahim has alleged that a DAP leader supported the idea of granting former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak a royal pardon and allowing him to serve his sentence under house arrest. In a Facebook post, Marina said she was tired of hearing the same political rhetoric repeated whenever elections approached, particularly over Najib. Marina alleged that the leader had hoped any pardon would only be granted after the general election to avoid undermining public confidence, particularly among the Chinese community.

items.title: VPCM refreshes Golf GTI with new rims
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Volkswagen Passenger Cars Malaysia (VPCM) has unveiled a refreshed look for its hot hatchback, featuring exclusive Huntsville Adamantium Dark rims as a complimentary upgrade for just 50 units of the Golf GTI. Featuring a striking dark finish, the 18" 5-spoke alloy wheel with unique Y-spoke design allows a clear view of the GTI's red brake callipers, enhancing the GTI's unmistakable sporty character. VPCM said the GTI 50 campaign is the brand's celebration of five decades of GTI heritage, marking the anniversary of its first release in 1976.

items.title: Unchecked AI progress may pose catastrophic risks, UN panel warns
items.summary: A preliminary report by the UN's Independent International Scientific Panel on Artificial Intelligence said policymakers face a growing dilemma: they need robust evidence to regulate AI effectively, yet such evidence is struggling to keep pace with the technology's rapid evolution. "AI capabilities are outpacing both scientific understanding and governments' ability to adapt," said Yoshua Bengio, co-chair of the panel, comprised of 40 cross-regional experts. "With growing evidence of deceptive AI behaviour, science currently cannot guarantee that as capabilities continue to increase, AI will not cause catastrophic harm, either on its own or due to malicious users."

items.title: Vietnam unveils 'baby bonus' after scrapping two-child policy
items.summary: A new population law and regulations coming into effect Wednesday extend maternity leave from six to seven months for mothers having a second child as well as offering financial help. If Hanoi residents Nguyen Kim Bich and her husband have a second child, she will get an extra month of maternity leave, free prenatal screenings and a small cash bonus. The new population law aims to slow the demographic shift.

items.title: CeX Malaysia taps Adyen for unified payments
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Global financial technology company Adyen has partnered with consumer electronics retailer CeX Malaysia to streamline payment operations across the retailer's physical stores and online platform. Since July 2025, CeX Malaysia has been rolling out Adyen's Unified Commerce solution across its e-commerce website and all 11 stores nationwide, enabling the retailer to consolidate payment management and reporting under a single platform. By adopting Adyen's platform, CeX Malaysia is now able to standardise payment reporting across its network, reducing operational complexity and minimising the risk of human error.

items.title: High Court dismisses Semantan Estate bid to postpone Duta Enclave compensation hearing
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 1 — The High Court today dismissed an application by Semantan Estate (1952) Sdn Bhd to stay proceedings to determine the amount of damages to be paid by the government regarding the value of the land known as Duta Enclave. On May 18, Semantan Estate filed an application to stay the proceedings until the appeal to obtain several documents including land acquisition plans between 1956 and 1960 to be used in determining the amount of damages to be paid by the government in relation to the ownership of the Duta Enclave land is decided by the Court of Appeal. On November 13 2025, the Federal Court dismissed Semantan Estate’s application for leave to appeal against the Court of Appeal’s decision that the Federal Territory Land Titles Registrar did not need to register 263.272 acres of land in Duta Enclave in the name of the company.

items.title: Unity Merdeka festival set to draw over one million visitors
items.summary: Held under the theme "United in Diversity, Sustaining Prosperity", the festival is organised by the Kuala Lumpur Police in collaboration with 16 ministries to foster unity among Malaysia's multi-ethnic communities. Kuala Lumpur police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said the event will feature activities focusing on community welfare, unity, arts and culture, education, religion, family and entertainment, and is open to people of all ages. Lee Traffic Police Station at 03-2071 9999, or their nearest police station.

items.title: Kelantan police hunt 'Mejoh' in Thailand over drug-debt abduction of two men
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Kelantan police have sought the assistance of their Thai counterparts to track down the main suspect believed to be behind the abduction of two men in Tumpat before the victims were found in Thailand on June 8. Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said the 40-year-old suspect, known as "Mejoh", is believed to be hiding in Thailand. They were found in Mundok, Thailand, two days later.

items.title: Cybercrimes Bill must protect journalists, whistleblowers acting in public interest, says MP
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Cybercrimes Bill should include explicit safeguards for journalists, whistleblowers and cybersecurity researchers acting responsibly to ensure the law targets criminals without undermining legitimate public interest activities. Datuk Ahmad Marzuk Shaary (PN-Pengkalan Chepa) said the proposed legislation must clearly protect responsible journalism, whistleblowing, cybersecurity research and legally privileged communications, including those between lawyers and their clients. "The bill must provide clear protection for journalists, whistleblowers, cybersecurity researchers acting responsibly, as well as communications protected under the law, including those between lawyers and their clients.

items.title: Bursa Malaysia reverses earlier gains to end lower
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia's benchmark index fell 0.43 per cent to close at its intraday low, reversing earlier gains as a wave of late-session selling erased the day's advances. At 5pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) eased 7.23 points to 1,656.83 from Tuesday's close of 1,664.06. Turnover decreased to 2.51 billion units valued at RM2.26 billion from 2.91 billion units valued at RM2.77 billion yesterday.

items.title: Pahang police seize over RM728,000 in drugs, arrest couple believed to be East Coast couriers
items.summary: KUANTAN, July 1 — Police arrested a couple and seized various types of drugs estimated to be worth more than RM728,000 at a hotel in Genting Permai, Bentong, on Monday. Acting Pahang police chief Datuk Azry Akmar Ayob said the suspects, a 37-year-old local man and a 35-year-old foreign woman, were arrested by a police team at the hotel’s parking area at about 5pm. He said searches of a hotel room and a car led to the discovery of various types of drugs, including an estimated 2.9 kilogrammes (kg) of ketamine, 1.01 kg of cocaine, 671 grammes of MDMA powder, and 538 grammes of methamphetamine.

items.title: Johor leads states with 8pct growth as Malaysia's economy expands 5.2pct
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's economy expanded by 5.2 per cent in 2025, with gross domestic product (GDP) reaching RM1.74 trillion, according to the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM). Johor recorded the highest growth among the states at 8 per cent, with its economy valued at RM171 billion, accounting for nearly 10 per cent of the national economy. Chief Statistician Datuk Seri Dr Mohd Uzir Mahidin said three other states also outperformed the national growth rate: Penang (7.3 per cent), Selangor (6.3 per cent) and Perak (5.7 per cent).

items.title: France introduces extra parental leave to boost birth rates
items.summary: PARIS: France on Wednesday launched new paid leave for both mothers and fathers, as President Emmanuel Macron seeks to boost the country's sluggish birth rate. In 2024, Macron unveiled a plan to combat infertility and the declining birth rate, saying it would help pave the way towards what he described as France's "demographic re-armament." Until now, paid maternity leave in France has been just under four months for a first child, while fathers have been entitled to 28 days of leave following a birth.

items.title: In Perak, prosecution rejects bid to drop charge against woman accused of trying to assault Sultan Nazrin
items.summary: IPOH, July 1 — The prosecution has rejected a representation by a woman seeking to have a charge of using criminal force against the Sultan of Perak, Sultan Nazrin Shah, during the state-level National Day celebration last year withdrawn. Deputy Public Prosecutor Nasrul Hadi Abdul Ghani informed the court of the decision during the mention of the case involving Nurhaswani Afni Mohamad Zorki, 41, before Magistrate Norizan Ridzuan, who fixed July 6 for case management. On October 8 last year, the same court rejected Nurhaswani Afni’s guilty plea after the prosecution informed the court that a report from Hospital Bahagia Tanjung Rambutan in Ulu Kinta here stated that the accused suffered from schizophrenia and was of unsound mind at the time of the incident.

items.title: Kelantan police chief advises Malaysians to delay non-essential trips to southern Thailand
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Kelantan police have advised Malaysians, especially those from the state, to postpone non-essential travel to southern Thailand after five shooting and bomb incidents were reported within a week. Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said while authorities cannot stop the public from visiting the area, regular security advice will be issued from time to time. "Currently, there have been five shooting and bomb incidents within a week.

items.title: Storms following heatwave kill two in Romania, Austria
items.summary: BUCHAREST: Storms that followed an intense European heatwave killed one person in Romania and another in Austria, authorities said on Wednesday. Storms lashed parts of Romania including the capital Bucharest, during the night from Tuesday to Wednesday, flooding some basements and homes and tearing down trees. Storms have also lashed Austria, causing landslides in Tyrol province and elsewhere since Sunday.

items.title: Budi Diesel rollout eases burden on private diesel users
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: Private diesel vehicle users in the Klang Valley say the nationwide implementation of the Budi Diesel subsidy has helped ease operating costs after months of coping with higher fuel prices. The subsidy helps us maintain those prices," he said. Hakim said his employer had previously switched to a petrol-powered van after diesel prices surged, as operating a diesel vehicle had become too costly.

items.title: Sarawak retiree loses RM914,000 to fake AI investment scam promising quick returns
items.summary: MUKAH, July 1 — A 56-year-old government retiree lost more than RM914,000 after being lured into a bogus online investment scheme that promised lucrative returns through an artificial intelligence (AI)-powered trading system. Mukah police chief DSP Muhamad Rizal Alias said police received a report from the victim at about 2.20pm yesterday. “The suspects claimed the investment relied on an AI-powered automated trading system capable of generating exceptionally high returns within a short period, convincing the victim to continue investing,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Expected next UK PM Burnham faces defence funding gap
items.summary: LONDON, July 1 (Reuters) - Andy Burnham will have to find an additional £4.7 billion (US$6.2 billion) to close a defence funding gap, either through deeper spending cuts or higher taxes, if, as expected, he becomes British prime minister later this month. Prime Minister Keir Starmer on Tuesday announced a long-delayed defence plan aimed at making Britain's depleted armed forces war-ready amid rising security threats and warnings that Russia could attack a Nato member as soon as 2030. Burnham is widely expected to become Britain's prime minister later in July.

items.title: Powering AI: Data centres set to consume nearly a third of Malaysia’s electricity by 2035
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 1 — Electricity consumption by the data centre sector is expected to increase significantly to 31 per cent — equivalent to 73,274 gigawatt-hours (GWh) — by 2035, up from 7 per cent or 10,544 GWh in 2026. The Ministry of Energy Transition and Water Transformation said the figures are based on projections by the Electricity Supply and Tariff Planning and Implementation Committee (JPPPET). According to the ministry, the rapid growth of the artificial intelligence (AI) and data centre industries has led to an increase in the country’s peak electricity demand.

items.title: Venezuelans search, suffer one week after deadly quakes
items.summary: A three-year-old boy was rescued alive from the rubble in Caracas on Tuesday, a Jordanian rescue team said, a full six days after Venezuela's most powerful quake in over a century. The rescues have come long after the 72-hour period considered critical for survival and as the UN refugee agency said "food shortages are widespread in hard-hit port city of La Guaira. "The situation is quite critical," said Lia Poggio, head of mission in Venezuela for the International Organisation for Migration (IOM).

items.title: Khaled: Malaysia to begin rocket production within two years
items.summary: CANBERRA, July 1 — Malaysia is set to begin rocket production within the next two years, with plans to eventually expand into missile manufacturing, said Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin. Mohamed Khaled added that Weststar’s investment will also allow Malaysia to plug into RTI’s global supply chain, opening doors for local players to thrive within the international defence ecosystem. Mohamed Khaled said the move is also in line with the National Defence Industry Policy, which aims to strengthen local defence capabilities and achieve sovereign defence standing.

items.title: Selangor Sultan credits Najib for LRT3, says project restored under Anwar, faults Lim Guan Eng over project cuts
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, July 1 — The Sultan of Selangor today credited former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak with approving the Shah Alam LRT3 project at his request in 2015, said the subsequent Pakatan Harapan administration had reduced its scope, and praised Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim for restoring key elements of the project. In a statement issued to mark the opening of the Shah Alam LRT3 line, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah said the rail project was conceived to ease chronic traffic congestion faced by commuters travelling between Klang, Shah Alam and Kuala Lumpur. “First and foremost, I would like to give credit to former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak for heeding my decree that a rail transport facility be built connecting Klang, Shah Alam and Kuala Lumpur,” the Sultan said.

items.title: Cybercrimes Bill targets users but lets AI companies off the hook, says MP
items.summary: Machang member of parliament Wan Ahmad Fayhsal Wan Ahmad Kamal said that while the bill has its merits, it remains insufficient to comprehensively tackle the issue. "The source of deepfakes continues to operate — the powerful AI companies that help people create deepfakes are still not regulated by law," he said during the debate on the bill in the Dewan Rakyat today. "We need a more established cybersecurity team in all ministries to address these new threats," Rosol said.

items.title: Police rule out foul play in death of autistic boy found in Perak drain
items.summary: IPOH, July 1 — Police have ruled out foul play in the death of a six-year-old autistic boy who was found in a drain at Kampung Baru Sungai Sumun, Hutan Melintang, yesterday. Bagan Datuk District Police chief Supt Mohammad Ali Mohd Jali said the conclusion was based on the post-mortem conducted by a forensic pathologist at Hospital Teluk Intan, which confirmed that the cause of death was drowning. Commenting on the victim’s disappearance, Mohammad Ali said police received a report on the incident at about 4.54 pm after the child was last seen in the living room of his house in Kampung Baru Sungai Sumun.

items.title: RM2.10 diesel among world's cheapest as Malaysia rolls out subsidy reform
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysians continue to enjoy one of the most competitive diesel prices globally, reinforcing the country's social protection framework amid persistent global energy cost pressures. Market observers said the subsidised diesel price of RM2.10 per litre under the Budi Madani diesel programme remains among the lowest globally, even as international diesel prices stay elevated and volatile. Eligible Malaysians can now buy subsidised diesel at RM2.10 per litre under the diesel programme effective July 1.

items.title: Saifuddin Nasution: LRT3 shows government commitment to modern, integrated public transport
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, July 1 — The Shah Alam Line LRT3 is another testament to the Madani government’s commitment to providing a more modern, efficient and integrated public transport system to improve people’s daily lives, Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said. He said the new line was introduced to ease congestion along one of the busiest transport corridors in the Klang Valley. On Sunday, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, when launching operations of the line, announced free fares on the Shah Alam Line LRT3 from June 29 to July 31, including Prasarana Malaysia Bhd (Prasarana) feeder bus services along the route.

items.title: No corn dogs? Trump's 'Great American State Fair' threatens to be a flop
items.summary: DESERTED booths, sparse crowds and prayer tents. Welcome to the Great American State Fair, where a celebration of the country's 250th birthday has morphed into a new political battlefront. Summers in Washington are notoriously steamy, but the proverbial heat has soared over the 16-day fair and how people view President Donald Trump's tight grip on the event.

items.title: Border Control and Protection Agency seeks Glock pistols for officers, cites durability and low maintenance
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 1 — The Border Control and Protection Agency (AKPS) has proposed the procurement of Glock firearms for its personnel, citing the weapon’s durability and low maintenance costs. AKPS director-general Datuk Seri Mohd Shuhaily Mohd Zain said the government has given its in-principle approval to supply the agency with firearms to facilitate operations and ensure safety during duty. Earlier, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim had approved an allocation of RM22 million to equip AKPS with suitable firearms and equipment.

items.title: Pas afraid to face DAP in Johor polls, says Loke
items.summary: DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke said Pas decided to consolidate the votes of its members behind BN since the party is aware of its slim chances, given that the majority of seats in Johor are mixed constituencies. "If they do not field candidates, their strategy is to consolidate the votes of their members behind BN, specifically MCA, in order to defeat the DAP candidate. "They (Pas) believe those votes will shift to BN," Loke, who is also transport minister, told reporters after attending a ceramah for PH candidate for Penggaram, Felicia Poh Rui Ling, in Batu Pahat last night.

items.title: Heartbreaking scenes as mother elephant refuses to leave dead calf after fatal road collision in Mersing (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 1 — An adult elephant believed to be the mother of a calf killed in a pre-dawn road collision remained at the crash site in Johor this morning, staying nearby until wildlife officers completed an operation to bury the young elephant and return it safely to the jungle. According to New Straits Times , the elephant calf was struck by a car along Jalan Felda Nitar near Kampung Pengkalan Bukit Asli, in Mersing. An adult elephant lingered at the scene, disrupting traffic as rescuers and wildlife personnel responded.

items.title: Malaysia to have rocket manufacturing plant in two years, missiles to follow
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is set to establish a local rocket manufacturing facility within two years under a strategic partnership between Weststar Defence Industries Sdn Bhd and Australia's Rocket Technologies International Pty Ltd (RTI). Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the collaboration would eventually expand into missile production, which would strengthen Malaysia's sovereign defence capability and reduce reliance on foreign suppliers. "RTI's expertise in rocket and missile technologies would provide Malaysia with access to advanced capabilities while allowing local industries to participate in high-value defence manufacturing.

items.title: Woman who tried to hug Sultan Nazrin must face trial after bid to drop charge fails
items.summary: IPOH: The prosecution has rejected a representation by a woman charged with using criminal force against the Sultan of Perak, Sultan Nazrin Muizzuddin Shah during the state-level National Day celebration last year. Deputy public prosecutor Nasrul Hadi Abdul Ghani told the magistrate's court today that the prosecution had rejected the application seeking the withdrawal of the charge. "Among other things, the representation sought the withdrawal of the charge.

items.title: What everyone gets wrong about the Petronas debate
items.summary: Most analysts predicted the worst — soaring oil prices, supply disruptions and a regional economic crisis. A key reason was Petronas. Petronas is different.

items.title: Standard Chartered loses bid to strike out US$2.7b 1MDB-linked claim in Singapore
items.summary: SINGAPORE, July 1 — Singapore’s High Court has upheld a lower court’s decision refusing to strike out a US$2.7 billion (RM11 billion) lawsuit against Standard Chartered Bank (Singapore) Limited over its alleged role in the 1MDB scandal, allowing the case to proceed to trial. The claim was brought by the court-appointed liquidators of three British Virgin Islands companies — Alsen Chance Holdings Limited, Blackstone Asia Real Estate Partners Limited and Brightstone Jewellery Limited — which they allege were used to channel misappropriated 1MDB funds. “We welcome the High Court’s decision on June 30, 2026 to uphold the dismissal of Standard Chartered Bank’s strike-out application,” the liquidators said.

items.title: Human Resources Ministry: RM64m paid out under Progressive Wage Policy to over 4,000 employers
items.summary: The wholesale and retail trade sector, including the repair of motor vehicles and motorcycles, recorded the highest participation at 26.7 per cent, followed by manufacturing (15.7 per cent), professional, scientific and technical activities (11.1 per cent), education (7.4 per cent), and accommodation and food service activities (6.9 per cent). “In terms of employer size, small companies accounted for 50.2 per cent of employers making claims. Among professionals, the median basic salary increased by RM350, from RM2,900 to RM3,250, representing a 12.1 per cent increase, while the average basic salary rose by RM364.36, from RM2,930.74 to RM3,295.10.

items.title: Customs Dept uncovers RM300.7m drug syndicate using fake food and household goods declarations
items.summary: NILAI, July 1 — The Royal Malaysian Customs Department (JKDM) dismantled an international drug distribution syndicate through five separate cases involving the seizure of various types of drugs weighing 4.83 tonnes, estimated to be worth RM300.7 million, from April to June this year. Its director-general, Datuk Amran Ahmad, said that this largest seizure involved 3,396.6 kilogrammes (kg) of heroin worth RM169.83 million, which was found in a container at West Port, Port Klang on June 15. He said three more seizure cases involved various types of drugs and psychotropic substances totallnig 6.11 kg, worth RM978,778, methylenedioxy methamphetamine or MDMA (220.3 kg) amounting to RM33.05 million, and cannabis buds (1,049.5 kg), worth RM83.96 million at various locations including KLIA and Port Klang.

items.title: Bukit Aman: Highway drug crackdown nets 135 drivers, with commercial motorists making up nearly 90pc
items.summary: Bukit Aman Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department (JSPT) director Commissioner Datuk Seri Muhammed Hasbullah Ali said the inter-agency operations targeting commercial vehicles were launched in response to a noticeable rise in drug use among drivers, prompting the ongoing enforcement initiative. “JSPT takes a serious view of drug use among commercial vehicle drivers, as it can impair concentration, judgment and vehicle control, significantly increasing the risk of fatal road accidents. Of the 135 individuals arrested, 121 were commercial vehicle drivers, while the remaining 14 were drivers of non-commercial vehicles.

items.title: Pakatan’s Sharon Teo rejects ‘parachute candidate’ label, cites years under Salahuddin’s mentorship
items.summary: JOHOR BHARU, July 1 — Her experience serving as a special officer to the late Datuk Seri Salahuddin Ayub has shaped the political values and approach adopted by Sharon Teo Siew Hui, the Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate for Permas, who has pledged to place public service at the centre of her agenda if given the mandate in the Johor State Election. Teo, 36, said she gained invaluable leadership lessons from the late Minister of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living, particularly in humility, accessibility and a steadfast commitment to serving the people regardless of race or background. Teo said accompanying the late Salahuddin throughout several general elections and state elections gave her a deeper understanding that being an elected representative goes beyond receiving complaints—it requires ensuring issues are followed through until they are resolved.

items.title: Court adjourns Rosmah’s solar hybrid graft appeal to September 1, says no more delays
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 1 — The Court of Appeal in Putrajaya today adjourned Datin Seri Rosmah Mansor’s appeal against her conviction in the RM1.25 billion solar hybrid project corruption case until September, adding that it would not allow any further adjournments. The Federal Court is scheduled to hear the review application on July 29. In allowing the adjournment, Justice Azhahari said the court had taken note of a letter dated October 10, 2024, from the president of the Court of Appeal instructing that the main appeal be deferred until the review application had been disposed of.

items.title: No Haj package scams reported this season, says religious affairs minister
items.summary: SEPANG, 1 July — No cases of Haj and Badal Haj (Haj by proxy) package scams were recorded throughout the 1447H/2026M season following integrated monitoring by various agencies, said Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Religious Affairs) Dr Zulkifli Hasan. “Alhamdulillah, we received no reports of fraud, including Badal Haj scams. Also present were Deputy Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Religious Affairs) Marhamah Rosli and TH Chairman Tan Sri Abdul Rashid Hussain.

items.title: Thailand, Malaysia eye September peace talks on southern border conflict
items.summary: BANGKOK, July 1 — Thailand and Malaysia are expected to hold high-level peace talks on the situation in Thailand’s southern border provinces in September, with both sides aiming to reach an agreement that could pave the way for a joint declaration. Wan Muhamad Noor expressed optimism that the peace dialogue was progressing in a positive direction. “We expect Thailand to ask Malaysia to continue expediting efforts towards achieving sustainable peace in the southern border region,” he said.

items.title: MACC officer selected to represent Malaysia at prestigious FBI National Academy in US
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 1 — Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Principal Senior Assistant Commissioner Irawan Khlir has been selected to represent Malaysia at the FBI National Academy (Session 299), an international training programme organised by the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) in Quantico, Virginia, United States. “Irawan’s participation in this programme forms part of MACC’s continuous efforts to strengthen officers’ capabilities through exposure to international operational standards, global best practices and more effective modern enforcement approaches,” the commission said. According to the commission, the intensive programme also includes modules covering strategic management, operational leadership, modern forensic approaches, intelligence analysis and technology-driven investigative techniques.

items.title: D-G: Malaysia’s border agency doubles seizure haul to RM140.9m in first five months of 2026
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 1 — The Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (AKPS) has recorded a total seizure value of RM140.863 million for the period from January to May this year, nearly double the RM82.1 million recorded in 2025. Its director-general, Datuk Seri Mohd Shuhaily Mohd Zain, said that the largest amount of these seizures involved Ops Green Shield, which is a comprehensive operation to detect electronic waste (e-waste) and operations at main entry points such as the Kuala Lumpur International Airport. Through the addition of human resources, Mohd Shuhaily said AKPS will also be capable to address border security threats more effectively, in addition to conducting its own investigations and intelligence operations.

items.title: Over 30,000 consumers approved to receive additional BUDI Diesel quota, says Amir Hamzah
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 1 — More than 30,000 consumers have applied for and received approval for an additional BUDI Diesel quota from 200 litres to 300 litres as of yesterday to meet the needs of those who use diesel in higher quantities. He said the improvement was among several initiatives implemented by the government to facilitate the implementation of BUDI Diesel which began to be expanded nationwide today. MOF also provides a mechanism for transferring BUDI Diesel eligibility for vehicles used by immediate family members other than the registered owner.

items.title: Popular conveyor-belt sushi chain Sushiro temporarily closes Suria KLCC outlet ‘following guidance’ from health authorities
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 1 — Japanese conveyor-belt sushi chain Sushiro has temporarily closed its Suria KLCC outlet from today “following guidance from Malaysian health authorities”, saying it is working to meet official requirements before reopening. In a statement posted on its official social media accounts today, the restaurant chain said the closure took effect on July 1. “Sushiro KLCC store has been temporarily closed effective 1 July 2026 following guidance from the Malaysian health authorities,” the company said.

items.title: Penang begins enforcing community service orders against litterbugs
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM, July 1 — Penang has begun enforcing Community Service Order (CSO) against individuals caught littering, as part of efforts to strengthen public cleanliness across the state. State Local Government and Town and Country Planning Committee chairman H’ng Mooi Lye said the enforcement took effect today under the Local Government (Amendment) Act 2025 and the Street, Drainage and Building (Amendment) Act 2025, which were gazetted on November 12 last year. Instead, immediate action will be taken against anyone found throwing, depositing, discarding or leaving litter in public places or on public roads.

items.title: Budget 2027 engagement sessions underway nationwide, says Amir Hamzah
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 1 — The engagement sessions for the 2027 Budget are now officially underway, with input being gathered from multiple ministries and locations across the country, according to Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan. Speaking to reporters after observing the BUDI Diesel programme at a petrol station here today, Amir Hamzah stressed that the government is taking a measured approach to ensure the 2027 Budget stays firmly aligned with the nation’s broader policy direction. For context, Budget 2026 totalled RM419.2 billion, comprising RM338.2 billion in operating expenditure and RM81 billion in development expenditure.

items.title: Wee Ka Siong rejects Pakatan claim that Perikatan is helping BN in Johor polls
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 1 — MCA president Datuk Seri Wee Ka Siong has denied claims by Pakatan Harapan (PH) that Perikatan Nasional (PN) is helping Barisan Nasional (BN) in the Johor state election. He described the claims as baseless, saying this was reflected in the four state seats contested by MCA under the BN banner. Wee, who is the Ayer Hitam MP, said the Jementah, Paloh, Layang-Layang and Stulang seats that MCA is contesting are expected to see fierce three-cornered contests involving BN, PH and PN.

items.title: Rapid KL says stray wire caused loud sound on Kelana Jaya LRT Line, rules out train fault (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 1 — Rapid KL said today that a thin external wire caught in the Kelana Jaya LRT Line’s power supply system caused a sound described as a “small explosion” that prompted a train to be withdrawn from service, adding that no one was injured and operations continued as normal. The incident occurred at 7.18pm yesterday, when Train No. 57 was at Taman Jaya station heading towards Putra Heights, the rail operator said in a statement.
